subject: Mozi Minute: How to Setup Profitshares
body:
Words I like: The only thing that's guaranteed about your business in 10 years is that you'll still be here. Everything else is conjecture.

Mozi Minute: Profitshares

Many employees want to participate in the upside. Few actually have the skills necessary to merit it.

They typically ask for equity without knowing the implications (taxes etc).

So I have a 4-part explanation that helps explain all the elements of equity and helps create something that accomplishes the same objective but protects them and you.

Profitshare = % of cash flow of the business as it operates

Sale bonus = % of money gotten from a sale in the future

Risk = they probably don't want this, but it comes with equity

Control = which you're probably not gonna give

Enter "phantom equity" or "synthetic equity". It goes by different names but the idea is the same.

Instead of agreeing on a valuation, and giving them permanent equity, which then they have to pay taxes on, which is often more than most can afford if appropriately valued…you just say:

"How about I give you what you want which is some share of the profits, and in the event we sell some portion of the proceeds."

Most people are down for this. Then you just need to talk about percentages and timelines.

Note: You can have a delay before these features turn on. This is called a vesting cliff.

Meaning, they don't get to participate for a year etc. then it turns on. And you can have it vest in waves. Like they get 1% per year for 4 years.

But here's the important part:

If they decide the company isn't for them anymore, or you don't think they're for the company anymore...

That synthetic equity comes back to you to give to the next person who is coming in to help you grow. And you don't keep bleeding portions of your business over time.

This way is fast and easy and doesn't trigger a tax event.

Note: just because you know how to do this doesn't mean you SHOULD. It's still a very valuable thing. After all, there are only 100 percentage points.

So if you even think it's worth doing, be smart about how much and to whom to give it to.

As promised, value in under a minute (ish).

You're not overexpanding, you're under-talenting,

Alex
